titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Mary and Agnes Berry Collection
titlestmt.author: by Lewis Walpole Library Staff

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 21:04

did.abstract: The Mary and Agnes Berry Collection contains letters to and from the sisters, primarily Mary Berry, and other material that sites them in their network of social, historical, and literary friends and colleagues. Many of the letters relate to Mary Berry's literary efforts, and her reading habits. Highlights of incoming correspondence are multiple letters from authors Benjamin Constant, Thomas Hope, Alexander William Kinglake, Sir James Mackintosh, and William Roscoe. Highlights of outgoing correspondence include thirty-two letters Berry wrote to the publisher Richard Bentley, eleven notes to Rev. Henry Hart Milman and his wife Mary Ann Cockell Milman, and four letters to Henrietta Browne, Viscountess Dillon. Series III, Other Papers, holds the sisters' bookplate, a poem by Mary Berry about Horace Walpole, and a poem to Berry by William Sotheby.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: W. S. Lewis purchased much of the collection from various sources. Bequest of Wilmarth Sheldon Lewis (Yale 1918), 1979.
Additional material has been and will be acquired from various sources. For more information see the finding aid.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Mary Berry Correspondence, 1798-1852. II. Agnes Berry Correspondence, 1845-1849. III. Other Papers, 1786-1788.

bioghist.head: Mary and Agnes Berry
bioghist.p: Mary Berry (1763-1852), author, was born at Stanwick, Yorkshire, England. She and her younger sister Agnes (1764-1852) began a close friendship with the author Horace Walpole in 1788; he spoke of them as his "twin wives." He established the sisters at Teddington in 1789, and two years later offered them the use of his nearby secondary residence, Little Strawberry Hill, in Twickenham. In 1824 the sisters took up residence at 8 Curzon Street in London, where they established a salon frequented by many prominent figures in society including William Makepeace Thackeray, Maria Edgeworth, and Madame de Staël. Mary Berry's literary productions include an edition of the Works of Horace Walpole (1798); the comedy Fashionable Friends (1802); A Comparative View of the Social Life of England and France from the Restoration of Charles the Second to the French Revolution (1828); and its sequel Social Life in England and France from the French Revolution in 1789 to that of July 1830 (1831). The Berry sisters both died in 1852, Agnes in January and Mary in November, and were interred in St. Peter's Churchyard in Petersham, London Borough of Richmond upon Thames. The inscription on their shared ledger stone was composed by public servant and poet George William Frederick Howard, 7th Earl of Carlisle.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: Prior to June 2023 the material in LWL MSS 50 had been interfiled in the Lewis Walpole Library Manuscript Miscellany (formerly LWL MSS MISC, now LWL MSS 36).
All material purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., in July 2010 was from a collection formed by A.M. Broadley in 1903.
All material purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers in November 2023 was formerly in an album of autograph letters compiled by Lady Theresa Villiers Lewis.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Mary and Agnes Berry Collection. The Lewis Walpole Library, Yale University.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Mary and Agnes Berry Collection contains letters to and from the sisters, primarily Mary Berry, and other material that sites them in their network of social, historical, and literary friends and colleagues. Many of the letters relate to Mary Berry's literary efforts, and her reading habits. Highlights of incoming correspondence are multiple letters from authors Benjamin Constant, Thomas Hope, Alexander William Kinglake, Sir James Mackintosh, and William Roscoe. Highlights of outgoing correspondence include thirty-two letters Berry wrote to the publisher Richard Bentley, eleven notes to Rev. Henry Hart Milman and his wife Mary Ann Cockell Milman, and four letters to Henrietta Browne, Viscountess Dillon. Series III, Other Papers, holds the sisters' bookplate, a poem by Mary Berry about Horace Walpole, and a poem to Berry by William Sotheby.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Mary and Agnes Berry Collection is the physical property of the Lewis Walpole Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the W. S. Lewis Librarian/Executive Director.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The material in Box 1, Folders 1-34 was purchased by Wilmarth S. Lewis; all material in boxes and folders following those was purchased by the Lewis Walpole Library, Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series I is organized into two subseries: Letters to Berry, and Letters from Berry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series holds letter written to and by Mary Berry, and forms the bulk of the collection. Some of her letters discuss literary projects, while others have to do with social engagements.

did.unittitle: Albany, Louise Maximiliane Caroline Emanuele, Princess of Stolberg-Gedern, calling herself Countess of, 1752-1824

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In French; an affectionate and friendly letter sending greetings to both Berry sisters as well as discussing medical remedies and visits from compatriots to Florence, including Lady Charlemont with her sister and her mother.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., July 2010.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

did.unittitle: Broglie, Albertine Ida Gustavine de Staël-Holstein, duchesse de, 1797-1838

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Broglie was the daughter of Berry's close friend Anne Louise Germaine de Staël-Holstein.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

did.unittitle: Brougham and Vaux, Henry Brougham, Baron, 1778-1868

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three letters from Paris, two in French, with a two-page undated manuscript essay on Berry's written works. Constant's essay was published in Lady Theresa Lewis's Extracts of the Journals and Correspondence of Miss Berry from the year 1783 to 1852 (1865) as "Criticisms by M. Benjamin Constant" (in vol. 3, pages 375-376.).

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Agar-Ellis offered his suggestions for edits to her Social Life in England and France from the French Revolution in 1789 to that of July 1830 (1831).

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

did.unittitle: Dudley, John William Ward, Earl of, 1781-1833

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ifan Kyrle Fletcher, March 1949.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ifan Kyrle Fletcher, March 1949.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ifan Kyrle Fletcher, March 1949.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two letters from Kinglake to Berry, and one from Kinglake to Lady Augusta Morley regarding a portrait of Berry.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ifan Kyrle Fletcher, March 1949.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ifan Kyrle Fletcher, March 1949.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ifan Kyrle Fletcher, March 1949.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An exchange of letters between Berry and Price, with her invitation to join her in Malvern, and his reply regarding the possibility of his doing so.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

did.unittitle: Richelieu, Armand-Emmanuel du Plessis, duc de, 1766-1822

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A note to Berry and a letter to an unidentified military officer about Berry.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two letters discussing Madame du Deffand's letters, his visit to the real Castle of Otranto, and offering praise of Horace Walpole.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Sharp wrote his opinion of Dr. James Graham "the most noted Charlatan of his time in England."

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Emily Driscoll, February 1961.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Stanhope was at the time styled Lord Mahon.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In a letter signed "Anacharsis" and addressed to his/her cousin General Sir Ronald Crawford Ferguson (-1841) in Kircaldy, Scotland, the author writes of an upcoming sea journey to Bremen, but most of the narrative is a somewhat sardonic review of the National Grand Jubilee held in Hyde, Green, and St. James's parks in London on August 1, 1814, illustrated by his/her small drawing of "A Volcano of Rockets." The event featured a "naval fight on the Serpentine" and a Chinese pagoda that was destroyed when it caught fire during the celebratory fireworks. The letter was readdressed to the Berry sisters.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letter from Whitehall, with indecipherable signature, responding to Berry's request to drive in her carriage through St. James's Park.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Jarndyce Antiquarian Booksellers, November 2023.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., July 2010.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Bentley was one of the foremost London publishers; Berry worked with him to produce her two-volulme 1844 work A Comparative View of Social Life in England and France.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Most of the letters were purchased from Maggs Bros. (Hodgson & Co. sale, June 1, 1945), June 1, 1945. The sale featured material owned by Lucy Rosamond Bentley, widow of Richard Bentley (1854-1936, the grandson of publisher Richard Bentley). Letters not acquired at the Hodgson sale were the gift of Lucy Bentley in 1937 and March 1952.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Twenty dated notes and letters from Berry; most discuss her work with him, social engagements, and the return of books she has borrowed.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Thirty-two notes and letters from Berry; all have month and day dates but no years. The contents are similar to the dated material above.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An empty envelope and cover, with manuscript note on a paper scrap.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Formerly loose in folio edition of Horace Walpole's Reminiscences .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four letters written between April 16 and October 5, with no year noted.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Brick Row Bookshop, November 1945.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance unknown.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., May 1962.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., July 2010.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Myers & Co., Ltd., April 1940.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With addressed envelope; she is possibly the wife of Sir Thomas Graves (c. 1747–1814) of Woodbine Hill, Honiston, Devon.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance unknown.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., July 2010.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Arthur Rogers, March 1955.

did.unittitle: Milman, Henry Hart, 1791-1868, and Mary Ann Cockell Milman, 1804-1871

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Eleven notes to Rev. and Mrs. Milman, some addressed to both and others to one or the other. Three note are dated 1849 and the rest have only months and days.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Brick Row Bookshop, November 1945.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., February 1958.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., July 2010.

did.unittitle: Richmond and Lennox, Charlotte Gordon, Duchess of, 1768-1842

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of H. Wade White, Waterbury, Connecticut, March 1952.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Thomas S. and Ellie Lamont, May 1964.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance unknown.

custodhist.head: Custodial History
custodhist.p: From the collection of Sir Thomas Phillipps, who added in pencil "1840" and "Miss Berry."

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of H.W. Liebert, August 1978.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., May 1966.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., July 2010.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., July 2010.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., May 1962.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance unknown.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance unknown.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series holds letters written by Agnes Berry to friends; the letters to publisher Richard Bentley were sent on behalf of her sister Mary.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Series II is organized into one subseries: Letters from Berry.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Four letters, three of which were sent on behalf of Mary Berry; the fourth thanks him for the loan of a book.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Two letters were purchased from Maggs Bros. (Hodgson & Co. sale, June 1, 1945), June 1945, and two were gifts from Lucy Rosamond Bentley, 1937.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Elliot was married to Thomas Frederick Elliot (1808-1880) and was the daughter of newspaperman James Perry (1756-1821).

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Myers & Co., Ltd., July 1955.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of H. W. Liebert, December 1953.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Perry was the daughter of newspaperman James Perry (1756-1821), and sister of Jane Perry Elliot.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In her note, Agnes Berry wrote of the July 25 marriage of Lady Georgina Augusta Kerr and Rev. Granville Hamilton Forbes, in Petersham, Surrey.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from John Wilson Manuscripts, Ltd., July 2010.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Winifred A. Myers, Ltd., May 1962.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series holds items other than correspondence, including the sisters' bookplate and two poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An engraved and etched bookplate for the sisters Mary and Agnes Berry, designed by Henry Tresham and engraved by Pietro Marco Vitali. It features two women in classical dress gesturing to a stone slab incised with the words "Miss Berrys." The bookplate is attached to a fragment of an envelope addressed to "Mrs. Bawden, Chard, Somerset" and date stamped 4 August 1813.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance unknown.

did.unittitle: Poem to and about Horace Walpole, by Mary Berry

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: An 8-line poem dated "Twickm. Common Monday night," and below in a late hand (likely Maria Brodie) "1788." For Horace Walpole's copy of the poem, see Horace Walpole to Lady Ossory, 1788 Oct 19. Printed in: Yale Edition of Horace Walpole's Correspondence 11, liii.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Provenance unknown.

did.unittitle: Poem to Mary Berry by William Sotheby, 1757-1833

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: In William Sotheby's hand and signed with his initials.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Ifan Kyrle Fletcher, March 1949.
